summary: The PoC successfully demonstrates template injection attacks including DoS via bcrypt CPU exhaustion and DNS reconnaissance of internal services using the getHostByName function.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should demonstrate that templates can use dangerous Sprig functions to cause DoS (bcrypt causing CPU exhaustion) and perform DNS reconnaissance of internal services.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully demonstrated multiple attack vectors: (1) DoS via bcrypt template causing 10-100+ second response times and CPU spikes, (2) DNS reconnaissance revealing internal Kubernetes service names, (3) Resource exhaustion via RSA key generation.
verification_method: The PoC verifies success by measuring response times (confirmed 10-100s delays with bcrypt), monitoring CPU usage (confirmed spikes to 80-100%), and capturing DNS query results showing internal infrastructure enumeration.
evidence_produced_by_run: DoS template with bcrypt caused response time increase from <1s to >100s
CPU usage spiked to 80-100% during bcrypt execution
DNS reconnaissance template successfully resolved internal Kubernetes services
RSA key generation caused memory/CPU resource exhaustion
Multiple malicious configs successfully deployed and executed
Template functions bcrypt, genPrivateKey, getHostByName confirmed available

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Heimdall repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
